title: Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve
tagline: Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Jayamangali Blackbuck Conservation Reserve is Tumkur district's only notified protected area.
overview.description: Jayamangali Blackbuck Conservation Reserve is Tumkur district's only notified protected area. It neighbours Talekere, a small village in Madhugiri Taluk, at the north-eastern tip of Tumkur district of Karnataka state, India. This area is a part of the plains of Deccan plateau and borders Anantapur district of Andhra Pradesh. It is a 798-acre (3.23 km2) patch of grassland with Eucalyptus and Acacia auriculiformis.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (73 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jayamangali Blackbuck Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adavinagenahalli, Kodigenahalli.
